intent: A first-time visitor heard about undavos.com from a LinkedIn post and wants to figure out what it is, who it's for, and whether it's worth any more of their attention.

success.criterion: After the simulated journey, would a fresh first-time visitor have a clear, evidence-backed sense of (a) what unDavos is as an event, (b) who it's for, and (c) why it has any credibility? PASS only if there is concrete content on the page (named themes, named speakers, named partners, dates, or a clear positioning statement) that supports all three. Vague generalities don't count.

notes: v0.3 alpha · llm_judged variant of acceptance-undavos-fresh. Where the saw_content version requires literal phrases (e.g. 'what we do' / 'who it's for'), this version asks Haiku to grade the journey post-loop against a prose criterion. Surfaces design-review judgment without brittle string matching.
